title: fan_out builds HashSet with target.to_string() allocation per follower
description: In delivery::fan_out, the dedup HashSet is built with seen.insert(target.to_string()) where target is either a shared_inbox_uri string slice or an inbox_uri string slice. The to_string() clone is called even for the first occurrence of each target before we know if it will be inserted. This allocates a String for every follower row regardless of whether it is a duplicate. Using a HashSet<&str> would avoid all clones until the data is bound into the SQL query (where an owned String is needed anyway). With thousands of followers this is O(n) avoidable allocations.
close_reason: Accepted: same pattern as zzj3.17/zzj3.40. Allocation per follower is dominated by delivery IO.
